preamble: The shift from the voiceless bilabial stop [p] to the glottal fricative [h] is a major form of debuccalization. It typically follows a lenition path where the stop first weakens to a bilabial fricative [ɸ] before losing its oral place of articulation entirely.

examples.note: The entire /h/ series in modern Japanese historically derives from a bilabial stop /p/.
PIE *p was regularly debuccalized and eventually lost in the Celtic branch.
